steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the two sentences
steps.content: The first sentence says the JWST is positioned almost one million miles beyond Earth's orbit. The second sentence says the JWST's predecessor, the Hubble, is only about 340 miles above Earth's surface. These two facts are sharply opposed — a contrast transition is needed.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Therefore,': Signals a logical consequence or conclusion. The Hubble's position is not a result of the JWST's position. ✗
  • Choice B — 'By contrast,': Explicitly marks opposition between two things. The JWST at nearly one million miles vs. the Hubble at 340 miles is a stark contrast in orbital distance — exactly what this transition signals. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Similarly,': Signals a parallel or comparable situation. The two distances are drastically different, not similar. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Secondly,': Signals enumeration (a second item in a list). The passage is not presenting a numbered list of facts — it is setting up a contrast. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
